interface: ARM REST API over management.azure.com with Microsoft Entra OAuth 2.0 client-credentials auth

form_fields.scope: https://management.azure.com/.default (resource identifier suffixed with .default; exactly one resource)

error_body.error_description: diagnostic text (must never be logged together with the client secret)

arm_request.api_version: every request carries an api-version query parameter; the value is operation-specific and the caller supplies it (tests use 2024-11-01 for the fictional pool resource)

async_operation.precedence_rule: use Azure-AsyncOperation when present; only when it is absent fall back to Operation-Location; only when both are absent use Location; a 202 with none of the three is a protocol violation and must surface as a structured client error, not a hang
async_operation.retry_after: integer seconds; when present the client must wait exactly that long before the next poll; when absent use the caller-configured default poll delay

status_monitor_body.status: required; Succeeded | Failed | Canceled are terminal; any other value (InProgress, Running, Accepted, provider-specific) means keep polling
status_monitor_body.error: object with code and message, present when status is Failed or Canceled

async_operation.location_url_semantics: a Location/Operation-Location monitor answers 202 while running and 200 (with the final body) when done; an Azure-AsyncOperation monitor answers 200 with a status JSON body and never carries the final resource
async_operation.final_resource: after Azure-AsyncOperation reports Succeeded for a PUT, the final resource is fetched with GET {original resource URL}?api-version={same api-version}
async_operation.provisioning_state: PUT/PATCH/DELETE bodies carry properties.provisioningState; Succeeded/Failed/Canceled are terminal, any other value means still running

error_contract.rule: the top-level error.code matches the x-ms-error-code response header when the service sends one
